vulnerability.title: Force frequent session key renegotiation
vulnerability.description: The RekeyLimit parameter specifies how often the session key of the is renegotiated, both in terms of amount of data that may be transmitted and the time elapsed. To decrease the default limits, add or correct the following line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf: RekeyLimit
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then var_rekey_limit_size='' var_rekey_limit_time='' mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*RekeyLimit\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*RekeyLimit\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"/*.conf if [ -e "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" ] ; then LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*RekeyLimit\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" else touch "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf" fi # make sure file has newline at the end sed -i -e '$a\' "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardenin

triage_decision.reason: The change involves adjusting the RekeyLimit parameter in the SSH configuration to enforce more frequent key renegotiation. This is a standard security hardening practice for SSH and does not impact authentication mechanisms or connectivity, provided the SSH service is restarted to apply the changes.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: Potential for minor increase in CPU overhead during high-volume data transfers due to more frequent rekeying

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan effectively addresses the vulnerability by centralizing the RekeyLimit configuration in a dedicated hardening file, removing conflicting entries from the main config and other includes, and ensuring the service is restarted to apply changes. This follows modern Linux configuration management patterns.
concerns: If the existing /etc/ssh/sshd_config contains conflicting RekeyLimit settings that are not matched by the sed pattern, they might persist, though the plan's regex approach is generally robust.
suggested_improvements: Verify the syntax of the SSH configuration using `sshd -t` before restarting the service to prevent service downtime due to configuration errors.
Ensure the user executing the plan has sufficient privileges (root/sudo) to modify files in /etc/ssh/ and restart systemd services.
